intro: I can help with listening live, audio problems, show times, requests, support and where else to listen.

fallback.title: I can help with common listener questions.
fallback.answer: Try asking about no sound, the play button, phone playback, Bluetooth, show times, song requests, support, accessibility, or where else to listen.

intents.title: How do I listen live?
intents.answer: Tap the Listen Live button near the top of the page. The browser needs one tap before it can start audio. If the built-in player does not start, use the Open Stream option in the listening section.

intents.title: Can I open the direct stream?
intents.answer: Yes. Open Listen Elsewhere and choose Direct audio stream. It opens the live audio feed directly, which can help if the normal website player does not suit your device.

intents.title: The stream is not playing. What should I try?
patterns: it says playing but no sound
i pressed play and nothing happened
it says playing but i hear nothing
volume is up but no sound
the website player is not working
intents.answer: First press Listen Live once, then check your device volume, browser tab volume, Bluetooth/headphones, and whether the phone is muted. If it still stays silent, refresh the page and press Listen Live again. If the website player still does not work on that device, open Listen Elsewhere and try the Direct audio stream or Online Radio Box.

intents.title: How do I change the volume?
patterns: how do i turn it up
intents.answer: Use the volume slider in the Listen Live section, and also check your phone/computer volume. If you are using Bluetooth, headphones, car audio or a smart speaker, check that device volume too.

intents.title: Can I keep listening while using other apps?
patterns: will it play with screen off
intents.answer: On some phones the browser may pause the stream when you lock the screen, switch apps, or use battery saving mode. Keep the browser open if needed, or try Direct audio stream from Listen Elsewhere. This is normal phone/browser behaviour rather than the station being off air.

intents.title: Which browser should I use?
intents.answer: The site is designed for modern phone, tablet, laptop and desktop browsers. Chrome, Safari, Edge, Firefox and Samsung Internet should all work. If one browser blocks playback, try refreshing or opening the stream directly.

intents.title: When is Jim Parr live?
patterns: what time does the show end
intents.answer: Jim’s usual live shows are Monday, Tuesday, Thursday and Friday from 10am to 12pm, plus Sunday from 10am to 1pm. Outside live shows, Coast Internet Radio continues with music around the clock.

intents.title: Is there a repeat show?
patterns: why does it say repeat show
is jim live or a repeat
intents.answer: The website can show a repeat-show status when a scheduled evening repeat is detected. Check the programme status near Now Playing for the current broadcast context.

intents.title: Does the station play all day?
intents.answer: Yes. Coast Internet Radio broadcasts around the clock, with live shows at selected times and music continuing outside those shows.

intents.title: Where can I see what is playing?
intents.answer: The Now Playing section shows the current track, Coming Up Next and recent plays when that information is available from the station feed.

intents.title: Where is Coming Up Next?
intents.answer: Coming Up Next appears inside the Now Playing panel when the station feed provides it. During live shows, the display may change because songs can be selected manually.

intents.title: Can I see recently played songs?
intents.answer: Recently played songs appear in the Now Playing section when the station feed provides them. This helps you check tracks you may have missed.

intents.title: Why does the song information look delayed?
intents.answer: Song information is pulled from the station feed and may take a short time to refresh. If it looks stuck, refresh the page. The live audio stream can still work even if track information is delayed.

intents.title: What does the listener number mean?
intents.answer: The listener number is a live estimate from the stream status. It can change often and may not include every listening platform or radio directory.

intents.title: How do I request a song?
patterns: how do i request a song
intents.answer: Use the Request a Song button on the website. You can also text song requests or messages using the number shown in the request section.

intents.title: Can I text the station?
intents.answer: Yes. Song requests and messages can be sent by text using the number shown in the request area of the website.

intents.title: How do I support the station?
intents.answer: Use the Support the Station button. It takes you to the station support link so you can contribute securely through the donation page.

intents.title: Where is the PayPal link?
intents.answer: The PayPal support link is in the Support the Station section and also in the button near the top of the page.

intents.title: What does support help with?
intents.answer: Support helps keep the station online and broadcasting for listeners. The website simply provides the support link; the donation page handles the payment process.

intents.title: How do I contact Coast Internet Radio?
patterns: how do i get in touch
intents.answer: Use the Contact links on the website for email, Facebook and X. Song requests can also be sent through the request form.

intents.title: Where are the social links?
intents.answer: The social links are in the quick actions area and footer. They include Facebook and X where available.

intents.title: Where is the current Coast website?
intents.answer: The current/original Coast Internet Radio website is still available while this updated site is being reviewed. Use the Current website button or the top banner if it is showing.

intents.title: Is this the updated Coast website?
intents.answer: Yes. This is the updated Coast Internet Radio website. It lets you listen live, request songs, check show times, use accessibility settings and open backup listening links.

intents.title: Which website address should I use?
intents.answer: Use coastinternetradio.com for this updated website. The current/original .co.uk website may also remain available during the review period.

intents.title: Can I listen on Alexa or smart speakers?
intents.answer: Alexa and smart-speaker listings are normally handled through radio directories or the speaker service itself. This website provides a browser player, but it does not control external smart-speaker listings.

intents.title: Can I listen through TuneIn or other radio apps?
intents.answer: Coast Internet Radio may also appear on radio directory platforms. Use the website player for the clearest current listening option, or use any directory link provided on the site.

intents.title: Where is Coast Internet Radio based?
intents.answer: Coast Internet Radio is live from Newry, Northern Ireland, and broadcasts online for listeners wherever they are.

intents.title: Who is Jim Parr?
intents.answer: Jim Parr is the presenter and station owner behind Coast Internet Radio. The About section gives a quick overview of the station and Jim’s shows.

intents.title: What music does Coast play?
intents.answer: Coast Internet Radio plays country, Irish country, American country, classic hits and golden oldies.

intents.title: How do I change text size or theme?
intents.answer: Use the Aa button in the header to change text size, contrast, font style, theme and motion settings. Your choices are saved on this device.

intents.title: Is there a light mode?
intents.answer: Yes. Open the Aa settings and choose Light under Theme. You can switch back to Dark at any time.

intents.title: Can I use high contrast?
intents.answer: Yes. Open the Aa settings and choose High contrast. This makes text and controls clearer for some readers.

intents.title: Can I reduce motion effects?
intents.answer: Yes. Open the Aa settings and choose Reduce motion. This limits movement and animation effects where possible.

intents.title: What privacy information is available?
intents.answer: The Privacy page explains what the website uses and what is stored locally on your device, such as accessibility preferences.

intents.title: Where are the website terms?
intents.answer: The Terms page explains basic website terms, external links and the live stream notice.

intents.title: How do I report a website problem?
intents.answer: If something does not look right, try refreshing the page first. If it still happens, contact Coast Internet Radio and explain what device you are using and what you were trying to do.

intents.title: Where else can I listen?
patterns: what if tunein does not work
intents.answer: Use the Listen Elsewhere button near the quick actions. The currently useful options are This website, Direct audio stream, Online Radio Box and the Original website. Some directory links are hidden when they are not playing correctly.

intents.title: Why does it say repeat show?
patterns: why does it say repeat show
intents.answer: That means a recorded repeat show is currently on air. During that type of programme, the source may show the programme name instead of individual song titles. Normal song details return afterwards.

intents.title: Why is the current website still available?
patterns: why is current website still available
why is old website still there
why is original site still linked
does this replace the old site
intents.answer: The current website is still available while the updated website is being reviewed. This gives listeners another familiar place to listen during the changeover.

intents.title: Is Jim live now?
patterns: is there a live show now
intents.answer: Check the Now Playing box near the top of the page. When Jim is live, it changes to show that Jim Parr is live now. Jim’s usual live shows are weekday mornings and Sunday morning.

intents.title: Can I send a dedication or message?
intents.answer: Yes. Use Request a Song and include the dedication or message in the notes. You can also text the station using the request number shown on the website.

intents.title: Does the website work on phones and tablets?
intents.answer: Yes. The website is designed for phones, tablets, laptops and desktops. On phones, keep the browser tab open if playback stops when switching apps or locking the screen.

intents.title: Can I still use the current website?
patterns: can i still use the current website
can i use the old website
intents.answer: Yes. The current Coast Internet Radio website is still available during the review period.

intents.title: Why does the stream stop on my phone?
patterns: why does stream stop on my phone
intents.answer: Some phone browsers pause website audio when the screen locks, battery saver starts, or you switch apps. Keep the browser open, try another browser, or use Listen Elsewhere for a radio-app option.

intents.title: Where are the show times?
intents.answer: The show times are in the Jim Parr Live section. Jim’s usual live shows are Monday, Tuesday, Thursday and Friday from 10am to 12pm, plus Sunday from 10am to 1pm.

intents.title: It looks like it is playing but I hear nothing.
patterns: looks like playing but no sound
play icon changed but no audio
button says pause but no sound
it is playing but i cant hear
no audio even though it is playing
intents.answer: That usually means the browser started the player but the audio output is muted, blocked, or connected somewhere else. Check device volume, tab volume, Bluetooth/headphones, then pause and press Listen Live again. If it still happens, try Direct audio stream in Listen Elsewhere.

intents.title: The stream keeps buffering or cutting out.
intents.answer: Buffering is usually caused by Wi‑Fi, mobile data, battery saving, or the browser pausing the stream. Try refreshing the page, press Listen Live again, switch Wi‑Fi/mobile data if possible, or use Direct audio stream from Listen Elsewhere.

intents.title: Can I listen through Bluetooth, headphones or in the car?
intents.answer: Yes. Start the stream on your phone first, then connect your phone to Bluetooth, headphones, a speaker, or your car. If sound comes from the wrong place, check your phone audio output/Bluetooth settings.

intents.title: What should I press to start the radio?
intents.answer: Press the large yellow play button in the Listen Live section. Browsers need a real tap/click before they can start audio, so the station will not usually autoplay by itself.

intents.title: Why do some radio app links not work?
intents.answer: Some third‑party radio directory pages can stop playing or use old station details. When that happens, they may be hidden from Listen Elsewhere until they are fixed. The main website player, Direct audio stream, Online Radio Box and Original website are the safer options right now.

intents.title: Is Jim on air right now?
intents.answer: Look at the radio status pill on the homepage. It shows "Live show with Jim" when Jim is broadcasting, "Repeat show" when an earlier show is being repeated, or "24-hour music playlist" outside show times. Jim's usual live shows are Monday, Tuesday, Thursday and Friday from 10am to 12pm, plus Sunday from 10am to 1pm.

intents.title: What's the audio quality?
intents.answer: The Coast Internet Radio stream is delivered at a standard internet-radio bitrate suitable for music playback. Audio quality depends on your connection and device, not the website. If audio sounds choppy, your connection may be slow — try the Listen Elsewhere options or refresh the page.

intents.title: How can I share Coast with friends?
intents.answer: Thanks for thinking of it. The website address is coastinternetradio.com — share that link with friends, on social media, by message, or by email. There's no special share button on the site, just copy the link.

intents.title: Can I read this site in another language?
intents.answer: Open the accessibility settings (the icon in the top right of the page) and tap "Translate this page". This uses your device's built-in translation tools through Google Translate. The original English text stays the master version.

intents.title: How do I send feedback?
intents.answer: Tap the "Feedback" link at the bottom of the homepage. A small form will open. You can write what you want to say, choose what it's about (website, audio, song idea, accessibility, or general). Your name and contact details are optional — only add them if you'd like Jim to reply.

intents.title: Does Coast have adverts?
intents.answer: Coast Internet Radio is an independent music station and does not run commercial radio adverts. Outside the live shows, music plays around the clock without ad breaks. The station is supported by listeners — there's a Donate option on the homepage if you'd like to help.

intents.title: Is it free to listen?
intents.answer: Yes, Coast Internet Radio is completely free to listen to. There's no subscription, no account, no paywall. The station is supported by Jim and by listener donations — if you'd like to help, there's a Donate option, but it's optional.

intents.title: How do I pause or resume?
intents.answer: The same large round button that started the stream also pauses it. Press it once to pause, press it again to resume. Your volume setting is kept when you pause and resume.

intents.title: What kind of music does Coast play?
intents.answer: Coast Internet Radio plays a friendly mix of music chosen by Jim, suited for everyday listening. It leans toward easy-listening, classic hits, and song selections Jim picks personally on his live shows. Outside live shows, an automated playlist continues with similar choices.

intents.title: Are old shows archived?
intents.answer: There isn't a podcast or catch-up archive at the moment. If you miss a live show, the station continues with its automated music playlist between shows. Live shows are scheduled at the times shown on the homepage, so check the schedule for the next one.

intents.title: I can't find a button or feature
intents.answer: Most controls are at the top of the homepage: the large Play/Listen Live button, and the Donate link in the header. Below that you'll find Now Playing, the schedule, and other sections. The Helper (the ? button in the corner) is always available. If something looks hidden, try scrolling, refreshing the page, or using the accessibility settings to increase text size.

intents.title: Can I open the radio in a new tab?
intents.answer: Open the homepage and start the stream as normal. The radio will continue playing in the background while you switch to other tabs in the same browser. On some phones, switching apps may pause audio depending on your operating system's rules.

intents.title: Will listening use a lot of data?
intents.answer: An internet radio stream uses roughly 30-60 MB per hour at typical bitrates. Over a long listening session on mobile data this can add up — if you're on a limited mobile data plan, listening on Wi-Fi is more economical. The website itself uses very little data once loaded.
